title: Ganga Chalisa
intro: Shri Ganga Chalisa - Hymn to Goddess Ganga
description:
॥ Shri Ganga Chalisa (English Transliteration) ॥

॥ Doha ॥
Jai Jai Jai Jag Pavani, Jayati Devsari Gang.
Jai Shiv Jata Nivasini, Anupam Tung Tarang.॥

॥ Chaupai ॥
Jai Jai Janani harana aghakhani. Anand karni Ganga Maharani.॥
Jai Bhagirathi Sursari Mata. Kalimal mool dalini vikhyata.॥
Jai Jai Jahanu Suta agh hanani. Bhishma ki mata jag janani.॥
Dhaval kamal dal mam tanu saje. Lakhi shat sharad chandra chhavi lajai.॥
Vahan makar vimal shuchi sohen. Amiya kalash kar lakhi man mohen.॥
Jadita ratna kanchan abhushan. Hiya mani har, haranitam dushan.॥
Jag pavani tray taap nasavani. Taral tarang tung man bhavani.॥
Jo Ganpati ati pujya pradhan. Ihun te pratham Ganga asnan.॥
Brahma kamandal vasini devi. Shri Prabhu pad pankaj sukh sevi.॥
Sathi sahastra sagar sut tarayo. Ganga Sagar tirath dharayo.॥
Agam tarang uthyo man bhavan. Lakhi tirath Haridwar suhavan.॥
Tirath raj Prayag akshayvata. Dharayo matu puni Kashi karvata.॥
Dhani dhani Sursari swarg ki sidhi. Tarani amita pitu pad pirihi.॥
Bhagirathi tap kiyo upara. Diyo Brahma tav Sursari dhara.॥
Jab jag janani chalyo haharai. Shambhu jata mahan rahyo samai.॥
Varsha paryant Ganga Maharani. Rahin Shambhu ke jata bhulani.॥
Puni Bhagirathi Shambhuhin dhyayo. Tab ik boond jata se payo.॥
Tate matu bhein tray dhara. Mrityu lok, nabha, aru patara.॥
Gain patal Prabhavati nama. Mandakini gai gagan lalama.॥
Mrityu lok Jahnavi suhavani. Kalimal harni agam jag pavani.॥
Dhani maiya tab mahima bhari. Dharm dhuri kali kalush kuthari.॥
Matu Prabhavati dhani Mandakini. Dhani Sura sarit sakal bhayanashini.॥
Pan karat nirmal Ganga jal. Pavat man ichhit anant phal.॥
Purva janma punya jab jagat. Tabhin dhyan Ganga mahan lagat.॥
Jai pagu Sursari hetu uthavahi. Tai jagi Ashwamedh phal pavahi.॥
Maha patit jin kahu na tare. Tin tare ik naam tihare.॥
Shat yojan hun se jo dhyavahi. Nishchai Vishnu lok pad pavahi.॥
Naam bhajata aganit agh nashai. Vimal gyan bal buddhi prakashe.॥
Jimi dhan mul dharm aru dana. Dharm mul Gangajal pana.॥
Tab gun gunan karat dukh bhajat. Grih grih sampati sumati virajat.॥
Gangahi nem sahit nit dhyavat. Durjanahun sajjan pad pavat.॥
Uddihin vidya bal pavai. Rogi rog mukt have javai.॥
Ganga Ganga jo nar kahin. Bhukha nanga kabuhun na rahahi.॥
Niksat hi mukh Ganga mai. Shravan dabi Yam chalant parai.॥
Mahan aghin adhaman kahan tare. Bhae naraka ke band kivaren.॥
Jo nar japi Gang shat nama. Sakal siddhi puran hvai kama.॥
Sab sukh bhog param pad pavahin. Aavagaman rahit hvai javahin.॥
Dhani maiya Sursari sukh daini. Dhani dhani tirath raj Triveni.॥
Kakara gram rishi Durvasa. Sundardas Ganga kar dasa.॥
Jo yah padhe Ganga Chalisa. Mili bhakti aviral vagisa.॥

॥ Doha ॥
Nit nae sukh sampati lahai. Dharen Ganga ka dhyan.॥
Ant samai sur pur basahi. Sadar baithi viman.॥
Samvat bhut nabh dishi. Ram janam din Chaitra.॥
Puran Chalisa kiya. Hari bhaktan hit netra.॥
meaning:
॥ Shri Ganga Chalisa (English Transliteration & Meaning) ॥

॥ Doha ॥
Jai Jai Jai Jag Pavani, Jayati Devsari Gang.
(Victory, victory, victory to the purifier of the world; victory to the Goddess of the Gods, Ganga.)
Jai Shiv Jata Nivasini, Anupam Tung Tarang.
(Victory to the one who dwells in Shiva's matted locks, possessing incomparable, towering waves.)

॥ Chaupai ॥
Jai Jai Janani harana aghakhani. Anand karni Ganga Maharani.
(Victory to the Mother who removes all sins. Queen Ganga, you are the giver of bliss.)
Jai Bhagirathi Sursari Mata. Kalimal mool dalini vikhyata.
(Victory to Mother Bhagirathi/Ganga. You are famous for destroying the root of all Kaliyuga's sins.)
Jai Jai Jahanu Suta agh hanani. Bhishma ki mata jag janani.
(Victory to the daughter of Jahnu Rishi, the destroyer of sins, Mother of Bhishma, and Mother of the world.)
Dhaval kamal dal mam tanu saje. Lakhi shat sharad chandra chhavi lajai.
(Your form is adorned like white lotus petals, seeing which the beauty of a hundred autumn moons feels ashamed.)
Vahan makar vimal shuchi sohen. Amiya kalash kar lakhi man mohen.
(Your vehicle, the crocodile, is pure and holy; seeing the nectar pot in your hand captivates the mind.)
Jadita ratna kanchan abhushan. Hiya mani har, haranitam dushan.
(You are adorned with gold and gem-studded ornaments; the necklace on your heart removes all flaws.)
Jag pavani tray taap nasavani. Taral tarang tung man bhavani.
(You purify the world and destroy the three types of suffering; your towering, flowing waves delight the mind.)
Jo Ganpati ati pujya pradhan. Ihun te pratham Ganga asnan.
(Even Lord Ganpati is highly revered and primary; yet, Ganga bath is significant even before worshipping him.)
Brahma kamandal vasini devi. Shri Prabhu pad pankaj sukh sevi.
(You are the Goddess who resided in Brahma's kamandal and serve the lotus feet of the Lord.)
Sathi sahastra sagar sut tarayo. Ganga Sagar tirath dharayo.
(You liberated the sixty thousand sons of Sagara; hence the holy place 'Ganga Sagar' was established.)
Agam tarang uthyo man bhavan. Lakhi tirath Haridwar suhavan.
(Seeing the beautiful pilgrimage site of Haridwar, your fathomless waves uplift the mind.)
Tirath raj Prayag akshayvata. Dharayo matu puni Kashi karvata.
(You are present at the King of pilgrimage sites, Prayag, near the Akshayavat, and at Kashi.)
Dhani dhani Sursari swarg ki sidhi. Tarani amita pitu pad pirihi.
(Blessed, blessed are you, the staircase to heaven; you have liberated countless ancestors.)
Bhagirathi tap kiyo upara. Diyo Brahma tav Sursari dhara.
(Bhagiratha performed great penance, and Brahmaji gave your stream as the divine river.)
Jab jag janani chalyo haharai. Shambhu jata mahan rahyo samai.
(When you, Mother of the World, flowed down, Lord Shambhu (Shiva) absorbed you into his matted locks.)
Varsha paryant Ganga Maharani. Rahin Shambhu ke jata bhulani.
(For a whole year, Queen Ganga, you remained forgotten/contained within Shiva's locks.)
Puni Bhagirathi Shambhuhin dhyayo. Tab ik boond jata se payo.
(Then Bhagiratha meditated on Shiva, and from that, he received a single drop from the locks.)
Tate matu bhein tray dhara. Mrityu lok, nabha, aru patara.
(Due to that, Mother, you divided into three streams: Earth, Heaven, and the Underworld.)
Gain patal Prabhavati nama. Mandakini gai gagan lalama.
(You went to the underworld named Prabhavati, and Mandakini went to the sky, the jewel of the heavens.)
Mrityu lok Jahnavi suhavani. Kalimal harni agam jag pavani.
(On Earth, you are the beautiful Jahnavi, the destroyer of Kaliyuga's sins and the purifier of the world.)
Dhani maiya tab mahima bhari. Dharm dhuri kali kalush kuthari.
(Blessed is your greatness, Mother; you are the axis of Dharma and an axe to the sins of Kaliyuga.)
Matu Prabhavati dhani Mandakini. Dhani Sura sarit sakal bhayanashini.
(Blessed is Mother Prabhavati, Mandakini; blessed is the divine river, destroyer of all fears.)
Pan karat nirmal Ganga jal. Pavat man ichhit anant phal.
(Drinking your pure Ganga water, one attains infinite desired fruits.)
Purva janma punya jab jagat. Tabhin dhyan Ganga mahan lagat.
(Only when merits of previous births awaken, does one feel devotion for Ganga.)
Jai pagu Sursari hetu uthavahi. Tai jagi Ashwamedh phal pavahi.
(Taking even a single step towards the divine river grants the fruit of an Ashwamedh Yajna.)
Maha patit jin kahu na tare. Tin tare ik naam tihare.
(Those great sinners whom no one could save, are saved by your name alone.)
Shat yojan hun se jo dhyavahi. Nishchai Vishnu lok pad pavahi.
(Those who meditate on you even from a hundred yojanas away, certainly attain the abode of Vishnu.)
Naam bhajata aganit agh nashai. Vimal gyan bal buddhi prakashe.
(Chanting your name destroys countless sins and illuminates pure knowledge, strength, and intellect.)
Jimi dhan mul dharm aru dana. Dharm mul Gangajal pana.
(As wealth is the basis of religion and charity, the basis of religion is drinking Ganga water.)
Tab gun gunan karat dukh bhajat. Grih grih sampati sumati virajat.
(Singing your virtues removes sorrow; prosperity and good wisdom reside in every home.)
Gangahi nem sahit nit dhyavat. Durjanahun sajjan pad pavat.
(Those who meditate on Ganga daily with discipline, even wicked people attain the status of the noble.)
Uddihin vidya bal pavai. Rogi rog mukt have javai.
(The ignorant attain knowledge and strength; the diseased become free from illness.)
Ganga Ganga jo nar kahin. Bhukha nanga kabuhun na rahahi.
(Those who utter "Ganga, Ganga" never remain hungry or destitute.)
Niksat hi mukh Ganga mai. Shravan dabi Yam chalant parai.
(As "Ganga" escapes the lips at death, Yamaraj's messengers flee covering their ears.)
Mahan aghin adhaman kahan tare. Bhae naraka ke band kivaren.
(You have liberated great sinners and the lowly; the doors of hell have been closed for them.)
Jo nar japi Gang shat nama. Sakal siddhi puran hvai kama.
(The person who chants the hundred names of Ganga, gets all their accomplishments and desires fulfilled.)
Sab sukh bhog param pad pavahin. Aavagaman rahit hvai javahin.
(Enjoying all happiness, they attain the Supreme State and become free from the cycle of rebirth.)
Dhani maiya Sursari sukh daini. Dhani dhani tirath raj Triveni.
(Blessed is Mother Sursari, the giver of happiness; blessed, blessed is the King of pilgrimages, Triveni.)
Kakara gram rishi Durvasa. Sundardas Ganga kar dasa.
(Sage Durvasa resides in Kakara village; Sundardas is a servant of Ganga.)
Jo yah padhe Ganga Chalisa. Mili bhakti aviral vagisa.
(Whoever reads this Ganga Chalisa, attains uninterrupted devotion and eloquence.)

॥ Doha ॥
Nit nae sukh sampati lahai. Dharen Ganga ka dhyan.
(One attains new happiness and wealth daily by meditating on Ganga.)
Ant samai sur pur basahi. Sadar baithi viman.
(At the end of time, they dwell in heaven, seated respectfully on a divine chariot.)
Samvat bhut nabh dishi. Ram janam din Chaitra.
(In the auspicious time, on the day of Ram Navami in the month of Chaitra.)
Puran Chalisa kiya. Hari bhaktan hit netra.
(This Chalisa was completed for the well-being of the devotees of Hari.)

faq.q1: What is the main benefit of reading the Ganga Chalisa?
faq.a1: Reciting the Ganga Chalisa purifies the mind, and it is believed to cleanse one's life of sins and bring deep positivity.
faq.q2: Can it be read every day?
faq.a2: Yes, reciting prayers to Maa Ganga daily is highly auspicious; it is very effective for mental purification and peace.
faq.q3: Is it necessary to read it after bathing?
faq.a3: Yes, Maa Ganga symbolizes purity, so it is best to take a bath and wear clean clothes before reciting her Chalisa.
faq.q4: Does the Ganga Chalisa improve mental well-being?
faq.a4: Absolutely; meditating on and reciting prayers to Maa Ganga reduces stress and helps one experience peace and wellness.
faq.q5: Where should it be read in the house?
faq.a5: It is best to read it while sitting in your home prayer room (Mandir), as the atmosphere there is calm and sattvic.
